title: The Lastest Gun In The West

jokes.text: A - ha! A free baseball!
jokes.setup: Character finding random objects
jokes.punchline: Excitement over mundane 'free' baseball
jokes.explanation: Simple character moment showing Bart's opportunistic nature, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: A shiny new dime! A shinier new dime!
jokes.setup: Finding one dime
jokes.punchline: Immediately finding an even shinier one
jokes.explanation: Nice escalation structure and economy, captures childish logic of comparative shininess

jokes.text: Could you possibly eat some free ice cream?
jokes.setup: Vendor's refrigeration unit is broken
jokes.punchline: Asking someone to do him a favor by eating ice cream
jokes.explanation: Clever reversal of normal vendor-customer dynamic, absurd logic presented matter-of-factly

jokes.text: Mom! A dog ate my clothes! Nice try, but we're still going to Riverdance.
jokes.setup: Bart claims dog ate his clothes (presumably to avoid going somewhere)
jokes.punchline: Marge immediately sees through the excuse, reveals dreaded destination
jokes.explanation: Perfect character interaction - Bart's creative excuse-making vs Marge's parental wisdom, plus Riverdance as cultural reference

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Learning he has to go to Riverdance
jokes.punchline: Homer's signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase perfectly deployed in context, maximum character integration and economy

jokes.text: You know that new baby brother Ralph's been bragging about? It's just a pinecone.
jokes.setup: Ralph has been bragging about a new baby brother
jokes.punchline: It's actually just a pinecone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor that captures Ralph's childlike confusion while being genuinely surprising and character-specific

jokes.text: Dad, it's a documentary on the homeless. Oh, right.
jokes.setup: Homer mistakes documentary for Tarzan movie
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual acceptance of his mistake without embarrassment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - the casual 'Oh, right' shows he's completely unfazed by being wrong

jokes.text: Really? Ooh! This I gotta see.
jokes.setup: Bart says a crazy dog is trying to kill him
jokes.punchline: Homer's excited interest in watching his son be attacked
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing Homer's terrible parenting instincts - his excitement about potential violence against Bart

jokes.text: Bart's a goner. Anyone want to be my new best friend? I will! Great! Finally, I'll be the dominant one.
jokes.setup: Milhouse needs new best friend since Bart is 'a goner'
jokes.punchline: Ralph volunteers, Milhouse reveals ulterior motive about dominance
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Milhouse's repressed desire for control and Ralph's eager innocence

jokes.text: Be quiet. Yes, sir.
jokes.setup: Milhouse immediately exercises his new dominance
jokes.punchline: Ralph's instant compliance
jokes.explanation: Quick payoff to previous joke, shows the dynamic working exactly as Milhouse hoped

jokes.text: Ha-ha! Bart's family is poor!
jokes.setup: Nelson making fun of Bart's family
jokes.punchline: Nelson calling another family poor (when his own family is notoriously poor)
jokes.explanation: Ironic since Nelson's family is famously impoverished, shows character's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Eat my short stories!
jokes.setup: Bart's usual catchphrase 'Eat my shorts'
jokes.punchline: Variation to 'Eat my short stories' (presumably for school)
jokes.explanation: Clever variation on Bart's catchphrase that fits the school context while maintaining the rebellious spirit

jokes.text: Hey, wait a minute. These animals are stuffed. Except that one.
jokes.setup: Bart realizes the animals are stuffed/fake
jokes.punchline: One real animal among the fake ones
jokes.explanation: Classic misdirection - sets up expectation that they're all fake, then subverts with one real threat

jokes.text: This one's from Gunfight at the Museum of Natural History.
jokes.setup: Buck explaining his movie props
jokes.punchline: Absurd movie title combining Western and museum setting
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of Western gunfight with educational museum setting, shows Buck's B-movie career

jokes.text: Who? Yeah, that's right. Buck McCoy.
jokes.setup: Bart doesn't recognize Buck McCoy's name
jokes.punchline: Buck's defensive confirmation as if being unrecognized proves his fame
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Buck's faded fame and desperate need for recognition

jokes.text: Who? Yeah, that's right. The David O. Selznick.
jokes.setup: Buck mentions David O. Selznick
jokes.punchline: Same pattern - Bart doesn't know who that is, Buck acts like recognition proves importance
jokes.explanation: Establishes the running gag of Buck name-dropping people Bart doesn't know with same defensive response

jokes.text: and I ain't not no un-dummy.
jokes.setup: Buck defending his lack of education
jokes.punchline: Quadruple negative that accidentally proves he IS a dummy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay where the character accidentally defeats his own argument through excessive negatives - perfect irony

jokes.text: He soils as much as he cleans.
jokes.setup: Horse is vacuuming
jokes.punchline: The horse defecates while cleaning, negating its usefulness
jokes.explanation: Perfectly balanced absurdist joke with elegant economy - the horse's natural function defeats its trained purpose

jokes.text: And directed one. And he got the 'film by' credit.
jokes.setup: Frank the horse was in 24 pictures
jokes.punchline: The horse also directed and got film credit
jokes.explanation: Great escalation from actor horse to director horse, satirizes Hollywood's treatment of animals and auteur theory

jokes.text: Hi, honey. Where's that vicious dog? I made him a biscuit.
jokes.setup: Bart coming home from dog encounter
jokes.punchline: Marge's motherly instinct to feed even threatening animals
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge maternal instinct applied absurdly

jokes.text: He was bigger than opium!
jokes.setup: Homer praising Buck McCoy's fame
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to opium rather than conventional measures of fame
jokes.explanation: Homer's bizarre comparison reveals his skewed perspective on what constitutes fame and importance

jokes.text: Then Six Brides For Seven Brothers.
jokes.setup: Buck listing his movie titles
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'Seven Brides for Seven Brothers'
jokes.explanation: Simple play on famous musical title, shows Buck's B-movie knock-off career

jokes.text: Just drinking, fighting and tripping horses with wires.
jokes.setup: Buck defending old movies as family-friendly (no drugs, nudity, cussing)
jokes.punchline: Lists equally problematic content that was apparently acceptable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Hollywood's selective morality - animal cruelty was fine as long as there was no nudity

jokes.text: Me-tal.
jokes.setup: Buck explaining everything used to be made of metal
jokes.punchline: Pronouncing 'metal' slowly as if it's a foreign concept
jokes.explanation: Shows Buck as living relic who treats basic materials like lost ancient knowledge

jokes.text: Everything tastes better when it's lassoed!
jokes.setup: Demonstrating cowboy trick
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous claim about lasso improving food
jokes.explanation: Absurd cowboy wisdom applied to food preparation

jokes.text: Now how the hell would I do that?
jokes.setup: Bart asks Buck to lasso him a banana after impressive lasso trick
jokes.punchline: Buck's practical confusion about the impossible request
jokes.explanation: Deflates Buck's cowboy mystique with practical reality - you can't lasso soft fruit

jokes.text: I guess you're not going to have an adventure this week.
jokes.setup: Buck can't deliver on the banana lassoing
jokes.punchline: Bart breaking fourth wall about TV episode structure
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about TV show expectations - Bart acknowledges the adventure format of episodic television

jokes.text: they're thinking of spraying your old man's workspace for ticks.
jokes.setup: Buck trying to change subject from his failure
jokes.punchline: Implies Homer's workplace is so dirty it has tick infestation
jokes.explanation: Casual insult about Homer's work conditions delivered as if it's good news

jokes.text: Horse-riding lessons, the finest makeup, delicious studio food.
jokes.setup: Homer jealous of Buck's advantages
jokes.punchline: Lists things that aren't really advantages for being a father/hero
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's skewed understanding of what makes someone heroic - focuses on superficial perks

jokes.text: On top of which, I don't look bald.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining why he's wearing cowboy hat
jokes.punchline: Secondary benefit is hiding his baldness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer vanity moment - his insecurity about baldness trumps desire to be heroic

jokes.text: Rattlesnake meat, varmint kabobs and refried whiskey.
jokes.setup: Marge listing Buck's favorite foods
jokes.punchline: 'Refried whiskey' as if alcohol can be prepared like beans
jokes.explanation: Absurd food preparation method applied to alcohol, good escalation from real Western foods to nonsense

jokes.text: Don't listen to them, Buck! It's an ambush! They're trying to jump your claim! Take him outside. I love you, Buck!
jokes.setup: Family trying to be nice to Buck
jokes.punchline: Bart treating dinner invitation like Western betrayal, dramatic declaration of love
jokes.explanation: Bart's complete immersion in Western fantasy, treating normal family interaction as villainous plot

jokes.text: One of the wheels broke off my chair today, but I didn't make a movie about it.
jokes.setup: Discussion about violence in old vs new movies
jokes.punchline: Buck's mundane chair problem as example of restraint
jokes.explanation: Perfect old-man complaint disguised as moral high ground about media violence

jokes.text: It only lasted a year, but we did 360 episodes,
jokes.setup: Buck's TV show only lasted a year
jokes.punchline: Somehow produced 360 episodes (more than days in a year)
jokes.explanation: Mathematical impossibility played straight, satirizes the ridiculous production schedules of old TV

jokes.text: Remember kids, Drunken Cowboy brand whiskey is smooth as milk!
jokes.setup: Buck doing commercials himself
jokes.punchline: Advertising whiskey to children with wholesome milk comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark satire of irresponsible advertising - targeting children with alcohol using innocent imagery

jokes.text: Well, that ad was aimed at children who were already heavy drinkers.
jokes.setup: Marge objects to selling whiskey to children
jokes.punchline: Buck's defense assumes children are already alcoholics
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that makes the situation worse - Buck's 'solution' reveals deeper moral blindness

jokes.text: It seems like all I did was shoot hippies.
jokes.setup: Buck explaining his 70s detective show career
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of targeting counterculture figures with violence
jokes.explanation: Dark observation about 70s TV violence targeting specific social groups, delivered matter-of-factly

jokes.text: The Western went the way of the evening newspaper and polio.
jokes.setup: Buck explaining the end of Western genre
jokes.punchline: Comparing entertainment genre to disease eradication
jokes.explanation: Clever juxtaposition of cultural change with medical progress - treats genre death like disease elimination

jokes.text: We can have showdowns at the schoolyard and use nerds as Indians.
jokes.setup: Bart suggesting Western revival
jokes.punchline: Casting social outcasts in villainous roles for playground violence
jokes.explanation: Shows Bart's casual cruelty toward unpopular kids, dark but character-appropriate

jokes.text: Hey, you're not fun, you're fat!
jokes.setup: Someone suggests alternative to cowboys (Hawaiian shirts)
jokes.punchline: Bart's blunt insult replacing 'fun' with 'fat'
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay insult, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm Kevin Costner in one of his Western roles! I'm a gulch!
jokes.setup: Kids naming Western characters they're portraying
jokes.punchline: Child claims to be a geographical feature
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation from people to landscape - child's confusion about what can be 'portrayed'

jokes.text: So I guess you could say this barely qualifies as news. I'm Kent Brockman.
jokes.setup: Kent reporting on children's Western craze
jokes.punchline: Admitting his own story isn't newsworthy
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on news media's tendency to report trivial stories as if they matter

jokes.text: Can't keep it in
jokes.setup: Parody of 'Don't Fence Me In' about bathroom restrictions
jokes.punchline: Homer can't control his bladder needs
jokes.explanation: Bathroom humor disguised as Western song parody, functional but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Oh, good, you worked in Judge Ito.
jokes.setup: Krusty receiving hanging chad sketch
jokes.punchline: Approval for including dated OJ trial reference
jokes.explanation: Satirizes comedy shows' tendency to pile on dated references - Krusty wants 'timeless' quality but approves topical jokes

jokes.text: They're dead? No, they just got lousy jobs.
jokes.setup: Buck says people who used reverse psychology are 'pushing up daisies'
jokes.punchline: Clarification that they're not dead, just employed poorly
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - sets up expectation of death threat, deflates to career disappointment

jokes.text: Let's see, Texas or Massachusetts? Texas.
jokes.setup: Choosing which gun to use
jokes.punchline: The obvious choice for a cowboy
jokes.explanation: Simple character consistency gag

jokes.text: Ooh! Right in the panhandle!
jokes.setup: Buck shooting at Texas-shaped target
jokes.punchline: Hitting the panhandle region of Texas outline
jokes.explanation: Simple geographical pun based on target shape - functional visual gag

jokes.text: And he's already given out all his cheers.
jokes.setup: TV Guide's Cheers and Jeers editor will be watching
jokes.punchline: Only has negative reviews left to give
jokes.explanation: Escalates Buck's performance anxiety - impossible to get positive review

jokes.text: We don't take kindly to transvestite chimpanzees here in Pine Corners!
jokes.setup: Western showdown setup
jokes.punchline: Absurdly specific and bizarre enemy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd combination of Western tropes with bizarre specificity

jokes.text: Oh! Like you're so perfect with your plastic mane and your painted-on spot. What's real on you? Nothing!
jokes.setup: Buck is drunk and supposed to save the day
jokes.punchline: Berates fake TV horse instead of acting heroically
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Buck's drunken rant at prop horse shows his complete failure as hero

jokes.text: Did I forget to mention I'm an alcoholic?
jokes.setup: Buck completely ruins TV appearance
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of alcoholism as if it's minor detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery treating serious addiction like forgotten trivia

jokes.text: This is horrible. My spit-takes all have blood in them.
jokes.setup: Krusty reacting to Buck's performance disaster
jokes.punchline: Physical comedy technique turned medical concern
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on classic comedy technique - stress is literally making Krusty bleed

jokes.text: Sorry don't suture my colon! And you're off my show forever!
jokes.setup: Buck apologizes for ruining show
jokes.punchline: Krusty's medical wordplay rejection
jokes.explanation: Clever medical pun while escalating to permanent banishment - Krusty's anger expressed through wordplay

jokes.text: That painting is made of liquor.
jokes.setup: Family removing all alcohol from Buck's house
jokes.punchline: Even artwork is alcoholic
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Buck's alcoholism - even decorative items are alcohol-based

jokes.text: Gold! Gold! Beautiful gold! Nuggets as big as your fists!
jokes.setup: Buck going through withdrawal/detox
jokes.punchline: Hallucinating gold rush fantasies
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate Western hallucination during withdrawal, fits Buck's cowboy persona

jokes.text: Well, I don't see how it is. Nobody's even told me your name yet.
jokes.setup: Homer insisting he has right to interfere with Buck's drinking
jokes.punchline: Buck points out they're basically strangers
jokes.explanation: Logical response to Homer's meddling - questions the basis for their intervention

jokes.text: A hero! A hero! A hero!
jokes.setup: Reporter saying police don't want anyone to be a hero
jokes.punchline: Echo effect emphasizing the word 'hero'
jokes.explanation: Simple repetition for emphasis, sets up Buck's heroic opportunity but not particularly clever

jokes.text: And you're no Howard Hawks.
jokes.setup: Buck refusing to help because Homer isn't a great director
jokes.punchline: Comparing Homer to legendary filmmaker Howard Hawks
jokes.explanation: Shows Buck's Hollywood pretensions - refusing real heroism because Homer isn't prestigious enough director

jokes.text: Whoa! There's nothing wrong with a little 'hey-hey!'
jokes.setup: Buck being disarmed of weapons before heroic mission
jokes.punchline: Defending keeping flask with euphemistic language
jokes.explanation: Buck's attempt to minimize his drinking problem with cute euphemism

jokes.text: I'm firing at the lasso, but the bullets just go through the middle!
jokes.setup: Bank robber trying to stop Buck's lasso
jokes.punchline: Can't hit rope because bullets pass through the center hole
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag about the physics of fighting a lasso - impossible to hit because of the hole in the middle

jokes.text: It's the ultimate weapon.
jokes.setup: Realizing they can't defeat the lasso
jokes.punchline: Treating simple rope as unstoppable force
jokes.explanation: Good escalation treating low-tech lasso as high-tech superweapon

jokes.text: Well, there's Krusty, Itchy, Scratchy, Poochie, America's Fire Fighters, and then you, Dad.
jokes.setup: Homer asking if Bart forgot someone as a hero
jokes.punchline: Long list placing Homer last after cartoon characters and abstract concepts
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Bart's honest hierarchy puts Homer dead last, even behind fictional characters

jokes.text: Goodbye, Bart! Never bother me again!
jokes.setup: Buck successfully completes heroic deed and reconciles with Bart
jokes.punchline: Immediately rejects future relationship with his admirer
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending deflation - Buck completes his heroic arc then immediately shuts the door on further involvement

tmdb_overview: While running away from a vicious dog, Bart meets Buck McCoy, a former Western film star. Bart begins hanging out with him and starts to idolize him. Bart wants to help McCoy stage a comeback, so he convinces all of the kids in town to become interested in the Wild West. McCoy appears on the Krusty the Clown Show, but the comeback flops when Buck begins drinking again and injures Krusty the Clown.
